raw_response:
I agree with the initial assessment provided by @Agent1. One key point worth emphasizing again is that while hyaluronidase (Option C) plays a supportive role by enhancing the spread of proteases like plasmin throughout tissues, it does not have the capability on its own to degrade fibrin. Its primary function involves facilitating easier penetration and distribution of substances across connective tissue barriers without altering structural proteins like those found in clots themselves.

Given this context, my agreement solidifies around option B being correct because only plasmin has been definitively shown to target and decompose fibrin components crucial for maintaining clot integrity. Thus, the chosen answer remains clear and unambiguous—**B**. Agreement confirmed. Answer Choice: B.
